commit.message:
feat(core): integrate .ai/packages/ into extension discovery

Refactor discoverExtensions() to follow Pi's pattern:
1. Auto-discover from .ai/extensions/ (local) and ~/.ai/extensions/ (global)
2. Resolve installed packages from .ai/packages/node_modules/
3. Resolve configured paths from settings.json "extensions" array
4. Resolve configured npm packages from settings.json "packages" array

Each subdirectory is resolved via package.json "dot-ai.extensions"
field or index.ts/index.js fallback.

Paths are deduplicated across all sources.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: add Holly same-day attribution filter to script 29

Pre-filter step removes IBKR positions where no Holly alert fired
for that symbol on the same trading day. Separates:
  - matched:    Holly alert + IBKR trade matched by time/price
  - ibkr_only:  Holly alert that day but no time/direction match
  - non_holly:  No Holly alert that day (manual/Finviz/other)
  - review:     Low-confidence match needing manual review

Against actual data: 476 of 4,558 fills (10.4%) had Holly alert
same day. Top non-Holly symbols: TSLA, NVDA, PLTR, AAPL, NFLX.

commit.message:
Fix video cut truncating end of match due to pre-match buffer

The OBS recording file includes pre-match buffer content (zone entry,
gates, countdown) but cutVideo was trimming to match duration from
offset 0, cutting off the end of the match by the buffer amount.

Only apply setDuration for clip operations. Regular recordings already
end at the right time since OBS is stopped after the overrun.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Haskell: O(δ log n) split-based compress via four-component finger tree measure

Add mMeanWeightSum (Σ mean×weight) as fourth measure component.
This enables O(1) weighted mean computation for any subtree, making
compress work by splitting the tree at K1 scale function unit
boundaries rather than performing a linear greedy merge:

  1. Compute q_j = k⁻¹(j) for each integer j in [k(0,δ)..k(1,δ)]
  2. FT.split at cumulative weight q_j × n — O(log n) per split
  3. Merge each chunk via measure: mean = mMeanWeightSum/mWeight — O(1)
  4. Snoc onto output tree — O(1)

Total compress: O(δ) splits × O(log n) = O(δ log n)

Four-component measure: (weight, count, maxMean, meanWeightSum)
  - weight: split by cumulative weight (quantile queries)
  - count: O(1) centroid count
  - maxMean: split by mean (insertion, CDF queries)
  - meanWeightSum: O(1) chunk merging (compress)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: pluggable solver architecture, local formula gen, client-side PoW

Miner GUI:
- Pluggable solver system: ProbSAT, AI-Guided, Pure AI, Custom script
- Local SAT formula generation via SHAKE256 (~866 SAT/s)
- Client-side PoW verification (DoubleSHA256 + target check)
- Only 1 RPC call per round (getwork), all seeds solved locally
- Balance display: available + immature (mining), auto-refresh on block
- Solver selection UI with dynamic config panels

Node RPC:
- getwork: returns header template + DIMACS formula, accepts address
- submitwork: accepts client timestamp to match getwork header
- Timestamp consistency between getwork/submitwork for PoW match

commit.message:
fix(ai-support): handle object-shaped device.entities in fetchDeviceContext

device.entities in memory is {0:{...},1:{...}} not an Array, so .map()
crashed with "map is not a function". Fixed by iterating fixed slots 0-3
using index access which works for both Array and object shapes.

  to ListStreamDestinationsResponse
- destination.go: update CLI to use PlatformUsername instead of Name
- Regenerated Go protobuf code

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: restore name field and UpdateStreamDestination for backwards compatibility

Keep both `name` and `platform_username` on StreamDestination — no
breaking change. CLI `dest new` writes to `name`; OAuth flow writes to
`platform_username`. Restore UpdateStreamDestination RPC.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: replace RenameStage RPC with UpdateStage using FieldMask

Use a general-purpose UpdateStage RPC with google.protobuf.FieldMask
instead of a dedicated RenameStage RPC. Callers specify which fields
to update via the mask (e.g. paths: ["name"]).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix landing page generation: use Python instead of sed

sed fails with multiline replacement in CI. Python handles
the template substitution reliably.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add scan log panel, inline error banner, custom toast/confirm dialogs

- web.py: capture all subprocess stdout+stderr into _scan_state['logs'];
  add GET /api/duplicates/scan/logs endpoint; reset logs on each new scan
- duplicates.html: replace browser alert/confirm with styled custom components:
  * Toast notifications (bottom-right, color-coded by type)
  * Confirm dialog (dark modal matching theme, used for deleteAll)
  * Inline scan error banner with "View logs" shortcut
  * Collapsible log panel (terminal output, auto-opens on scan error,
    polls during scan, color-codes errors/warnings/success lines)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Task 71: feat: add soft pause and task content hash validation (#76)

* feat: add soft pause and task content hash validation

Part 1: Soft pause support
- Empty PAUSE file = soft pause: current phase completes, then stops
- PAUSE containing "NOW" = hard pause: exits immediately (old behavior)
- check_soft_pause() called after _handle_pending, _handle_reviewed,
  _handle_fixed, _handle_test_fixing phase completion
- Crash recovery handlers (_handle_implementing, _handle_fixing,
  _handle_merging) and _handle_pr_open still run during soft pause

Part 2: Task content validation
- Hash task body on branch creation, store as task_content_hash in state
- Re-hash before coder and fixer spawn, warn if content changed
- _compute_hash() for cross-platform MD5 (macOS md5, Linux md5sum)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add soft pause and task hash tests, fix existing pause tests

New test files:
- test_soft_pause.bats: hard/soft pause behavior, check_soft_pause exit
- test_task_hash.bats: hash consistency, match/mismatch, storage in state

Updated:
- test_entry_common.bats: update PAUSE tests for new soft/hard semantics
- dispatcher.sh: source entry-common.sh for check_soft_pause availability

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address PR review feedback for soft pause and task hash

1. PAUSE file: any non-empty content = hard pause (backward compatible).
   Only empty file = soft pause. Previously only "NOW" was hard pause,
   which silently changed behavior for existing PAUSE files with text.

2. Remove no-op _check_task_content_hash call in _handle_pending — the
   hash was computed 12 lines earlier in the same invocation, so the
   check always matched.

3. Add tests for arbitrary non-empty PAUSE content ("paused for
   maintenance", "STOP") confirming hard pause behavior.

4. Remove check_soft_pause from _handle_test_fixing — test-fix retries
   are part of the current task's phase, not new work. With soft pause
   the handler would re-enter and exit on every tick, preventing retries
   from ever completing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

with auth token resolution.

commit.message:
fix: ensure unwatchGame runs on game completion and skip polling Finished games

handleGameComplete now wraps commit/resolve/notify in try/finally so that
cleanup (unwatchGame, map deletions) always executes even on failure.
Previously, a commit failure caused an early return that left Finished games
polling TEE RPC every 3s indefinitely, triggering 429 rate limits.

Also adds a Finished-phase guard in forcePoll as an additional safeguard.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: Supabase + NocoDB architecture sketch for task management

Add docs/SUPABASE-NOCODB-SKETCH.md covering:
- Schema design (tasks, task_links junction, research_runs, strategy_notes, agent_sessions)
- Supabase setup (Postgres, RLS, auto-REST, real-time)
- NocoDB integration (spreadsheet UI + MCP server for agent CRUD)
- Data flow: local SQLite/DuckDB stays, cloud Postgres for task coordination
- Migration path from FEATURE-PLAN.md → tasks table with seed SQL
- Implementation steps (~1h setup, +3h for MDB server integration)

Update MEMORY.md with Silver normalization stats, sizing simulation findings,
and Supabase/NocoDB planning status.
